title: Chapter 5 Quiz
description: Test your knowledge of Chapter 5 concepts!

questions.question: What is the primary purpose of a loop?
options: To execute a block of code repeatedly as long as a condition is true
questions.explanation: Loops automate repetitive tasks by repeating code.

questions.question: A loop that never stops is called an _____ loop.
questions.explanation: If the condition never evaluates to false, it loops to infinity, freezing the program.

questions.question: A while loop will execute its block at least once, even if the condition is false initially.
questions.explanation: No, a regular while loop checks the condition BEFORE running. If it's false originally, it skips the block entirely.

questions.question: Which loop guarantees that its code block will be executed AT LEAST ONCE?
questions.explanation: A do-while loop executes code and THEN checks the condition at the end.

options: It will cause an infinite loop.
questions.explanation: There is no i++ or any instruction to change the condition, so i remains 0 forever, making i < 5 always true.

questions.question: When you know exactly how many times you want to loop, which loop should you use?
questions.explanation: A for loop bundles the setup, condition, and incrementing together on one line.

questions.question: In `for(int i = 0; i < 5; i++)`, `int i = 0` only executes _____ time(s).
questions.explanation: The first statement is for declaration and runs exactly once before the loop begins.

questions.question: Which statement is used to jump completely out of a loop?
questions.explanation: The block's execution completely halts when hitting a break keyword.

questions.question: Which statement skips the current iteration and jumps to the next cycle of the loop?
questions.explanation: The continue statement ignores the rest of the current block, but doesn't destroy the loop.

questions.question: In a nested loop, the inner loop must complete all its cycles for every single outer loop iteration.
questions.explanation: Yes, if the outer runs 2 times and inner runs 3 times, the inner loop code ultimately fires 6 times.

questions.question:
How many values will this print?
for (int i = 1; i <= 3; i++) { }
questions.explanation: It prints 1, 2, and 3. So total prints is 3.
do-while runs the code once before checking. Since 10 < 5 is false, it stops after printing '10' once.

questions.question: To use a for loop counting downwards, you usually update the condition with _____ (decrement operator).
questions.explanation: i-- subtracts 1.

questions.question: A typical while loop and a for loop can essentially achieve the exact same thing.
questions.explanation: They perform the same logic, just visually organized differently.

questions.question: What is the proper syntax for a do-while loop?
questions.explanation: A do-while loop requires a trailing semicolon: do { ... } while (condition);

questions.question: In `for(int i = 0; i < 5; i++)`, the `i++` part executes _____ the code block runs.
questions.explanation: The update sequence happens at the end of each iteration.

questions.question: Can you put a while loop inside a for loop?
options: No, they must be the same block type.
Yes, any loop can be nested in any loop.
Yes, but only once per file.
questions.explanation: You can mix and match nesting types without issue.

questions.question: The continue statement can be used inside a `switch` statement instead of `break`.
questions.explanation: No, `continue` is specifically designed for loops, not standard switch cases.
Since `i` is never updated inside the loop, it remains 0 forever, creating an infinite loop.

questions.question: To get out of an infinite loop early during execution, use _____.
questions.explanation: The break command immediately ends the nearest loop block.
